**Research conclusion (from established kinetics theory, since tools returned nothing usable):** The canonical Knowles/Cohen integrated rate law for nucleation-and-growth amyloid kinetics produces a sigmoidal aggregate-mass curve with three signatures: (1) a flat lag phase, (2) a genuine interior inflection during elongation, and (3) a saturating plateau. The half-time scales as a power law of monomer concentration, and seeding shortens the lag. A logistic sigmoid placed on a monomer-scaled, seed-accelerated effective time axis reproduces all three signatures with a freely-located inflection — which the stretched-exponential parent (0.9199) cannot represent, since its converted-fraction form has a constrained second-derivative profile and lacks a tr
